notes: Ancient Greek lyric poet known for his virtuous character and thoughtful deliberation on the nature of the gods.

docs.title: BOOK XXIX
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: An information had been laid against him as having been made aware of what was going on by Fidustius, as he saw that his cause depended, not on its truth, but on the will of one man, avowed that he had known all that was

docs.title: Octavius
docs.author: Minucius Felix
docs.excerpt: … to be, asked first of all for a day to deliberate; then postponed his reply for two days; and then, when pressed, he added only another; and finally, when the tyrant inquired into the causes of such a long delay, he replied that, the longer his research continued, the obscurer the truth became to
